page.content:
The data presented in the table elucidates the treatment parameters and outcomes associated with the Gross Tumor Volume of the neck (GTVnx) in patients undergoing radiotherapy. A critical target for radiation dose delivery is established at 68 Gy, which serves as the benchmark for evaluating therapeutic effectiveness. Notably, the median volume of the tumor, measured at 2.47 cm³, is indicative of the size and potential complexity of treatment planning. 

Furthermore, the median minimal dose recorded is 65.57 Gy, while the median maximal dose reaches 81.24 Gy, reflecting the variability in dose distribution that may be encountered during treatment.

page.content: The accompanying data on median values and standard deviations for various metrics, including the questionnaire responses, provides insight into patient-reported outcomes and quality of life post-treatment. The statistical analysis, as indicated by the One-way ANOVA, allows for a robust examination of differences among various treatment cohorts, facilitating a deeper understanding of the correlation between dose parameters and patient outcomes in this clinical setting.
